name: incident-runbook-templates
description: Create structured incident response runbooks with step-by-step procedures, escalation paths, and recovery actions. Use when building runbooks, responding to incidents, or establishing incident response procedures.
capabilities: Provide a structured incident runbook outline with required sections
Define incident severity levels with impact and response time guidance
Offer a service outage runbook with triage and mitigation steps
Offer a database incident runbook with SQL checks and actions
Provide escalation matrix and communication template examples
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only static markdown templates. No executable code, no file system access, no network calls. All 70 static findings are false positives: markdown code blocks were misidentified as Ruby/shell backticks, example URLs were flagged as hardcoded network addresses, and SQL/YAML content was misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms. The skill generates text templates only.
valueStatement: Teams struggle to document consistent incident response steps during pressure. This skill delivers structured runbook templates with triage, mitigation, and communication sections.
limitations: Does not execute commands or verify results
Assumes Kubernetes and PostgreSQL in examples
Requires customization for your services and contacts
Does not provide monitoring or alerting setup

useCases.title: Handle a live outage
useCases.description: Follow structured triage and mitigation steps while keeping stakeholders informed.

useCases.title: Standardize runbooks
useCases.description: Create consistent runbook structure and severity levels across teams.

useCases.title: Learn incident flow
useCases.description: Study templates to understand escalation paths and recovery procedures.

promptTemplates.title: Runbook outline
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a concise incident runbook outline for a SaaS API service with detection, triage, mitigation, verification, and communication sections.

promptTemplates.title: Service outage guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Draft a service outage runbook for a payments API. Include triage steps, rollback actions, and escalation contacts.

promptTemplates.title: Database incident guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a database incident runbook for PostgreSQL with connection exhaustion, replication lag, and disk space issues.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced playbook
promptTemplates.prompt: Extend a runbook with specific alerts, dashboards, and verification metrics for a microservice on Kubernetes.

bestPractices: Customize commands, dashboards, and contacts before use
Order steps by urgency and verify each action
Review and update templates after incidents
antiPatterns: Leaving placeholders without owners or contacts
Including steps that require undocumented tribal knowledge

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The content is plain text templates that work in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What are the limits of this skill?
faq.answer: It provides guidance only and does not execute commands or monitor systems.

faq.question: Can I integrate it with our incident tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Replace placeholders with your PagerDuty, Slack, and dashboard links.

faq.question: Does it access or store sensitive data?
faq.answer: No. It is static content and does not read files or send data.

faq.question: What if the steps do not match our stack?
faq.answer: Edit the commands and sections to match your services and tooling.

faq.question: How does this compare to incident platforms?
faq.answer: It offers templates only, while platforms add automation and live coordination.
